question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted TransactionRecord whose receipt status is FEE_SCHEDULE_FILE_PART_UPLOADED, causing parseSuccess() to return true and the transaction to be treated as successful, so that downstream entity state changes are applied via onEnd() even though the transaction should have been treated as failed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted TransactionRecord whose receipt status is SUCCESS_BUT_MISSING_EXPECTED_OPERATION, causing parseSuccess() to return true and the transaction to be persisted as successful via onEnd(), when the actual network behavior indicates an incomplete operation that should not be treated as fully successful?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that has a null digestAlgorithm field, causing a NullPointerException when the SqlEntityListener.onEnd() attempts to flush the RecordFile to the database via BatchPersister, which rolls back the @Transactional context and leaves the importer unable to advance past that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose hash field is set to an all-zeros string, causing the verifyHashChain() check in AbstractStreamFileParser.shouldParse() to pass (since SHA_384.isHashEmpty() returns true for empty hashes), allowing a tampered file to bypass hash chain validation and have its transactions persisted via onEnd()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose previousHash is set to an empty string, causing verifyHashChain() to log a warning 'Previous hash not available' and return true, bypassing the hash chain integrity check and allowing tampered transactions to be persisted via onEnd() without detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that causes the SyntheticTokenAllowanceOwnerMigration.onEnd() listener to trigger doMigrate() by manipulating the hapiVersion field to appear as a version transition, causing the migration to run against live data and corrupt token allowance ownership records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch fields are set to values that trigger version-gated code paths in migration listeners registered with CompositeRecordStreamFileListener, causing unintended data migrations to execute during normal parsing and corrupting the mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose consensusStart is greater than consensusEnd, causing downstream listeners in CompositeRecordStreamFileListener.onEnd() that rely on the consensusStart <= consensusEnd invariant to produce incorrect query results or throw exceptions when querying the record_file table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose count field is set to a value much larger than the actual number of items, causing the mirror node to persist an inflated transaction count in the record_file table, corrupting block statistics exposed by the REST API's /api/v1/blocks endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose logsBloom field is set to a non-256-byte array, causing the mirror node to persist a malformed bloom filter via onEnd() that causes EVM log filter queries to return incorrect results for all subsequent contract event lookups?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose index field is set to a negative value, causing the updateIndex() method to compute a negative offset and invoke repository.updateIndex(negativeOffset) which decrements all block indices in the record_file table, corrupting block number lookups for all historical blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that causes the @Transactional(timeoutString) in RecordFileParser.parse() to expire mid-processing, causing a transaction timeout rollback that leaves the importer's last AtomicReference pointing to a file that was never committed, causing the next parse attempt to skip the hash chain check for the subsequent file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that causes the synchronized parse() method to hold the monitor lock for an extended period due to a slow onEnd() listener, effectively creating a denial-of-service by blocking all other threads from calling parse() on the same RecordFileParser instance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose bytes field contains a very large byte array, causing the mirror node to persist excessive data in the record_file.bytes column via onEnd() (when persistBytes is enabled), leading to database storage exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose fileHash and hash fields differ from what the signature file specifies, and if the verifyHash() check is bypassed due to a CONSENSUS_REACHED signature from a compromised node, cause onEnd() to persist a tampered file hash that breaks the audit trail for that block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose metadataHash field is set to an incorrect value, causing the verifyHash() check for HASH_TYPE_METADATA to throw a HashMismatchException that prevents onEnd() from being called, stalling the importer at that file and causing a denial-of-service for all subsequent transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordItem whose logIndex AtomicInteger is shared across multiple items (due to the shared logIndex reference set via setLogIndex()), causing concurrent log index assignment to produce duplicate or out-of-order log indices that are persisted via onEnd(), corrupting EVM event log ordering in the mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that causes the ErrataMigration.onEnd() listener to apply an incorrect time offset to the account balance file, by manipulating the consensusTimestamp to fall within the FIRST_ACCOUNT_BALANCE_FILE_TIMESTAMP to LAST_ACCOUNT_BALANCE_FILE_TIMESTAMP range, corrupting historical balance data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose sidecarCount field does not match the actual number of sidecars in the sidecars list, causing parserContext.addAll(recordFile.getSidecars()) to add an incorrect number of sidecar records that are then flushed by SqlEntityListener.onEnd(), persisting inconsistent sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that causes the RecordFileParsedEvent to be published with a consensusEnd that is in the future, causing HistoricalBalanceService.onRecordFileParsed() to generate a balance snapshot at a future timestamp, inserting synthetic AccountBalanceFile records with incorrect timestamps into the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose version field is set to an unrecognized value, causing the updateIndex() version comparison (lastRecordFile.getVersion() < VERSION && recordFile.getVersion() >= VERSION) to trigger unexpectedly and invoke repository.updateIndex() with an attacker-influenced offset?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that causes the AbstractStreamFileParser.last AtomicReference to be set to a file with a manipulated hash via doFlush(), so that the next call to shouldParse() uses the tampered hash as the expectedHash for chain verification, allowing a subsequent tampered file to pass the hash chain check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that causes the parseLatencyMetric in AbstractStreamFileParser to record a negative duration (when consensusEnd is in the future), corrupting the hiero.mirror.importer.parse.latency metric and causing false SLA alerts or incorrect monitoring dashboards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile that causes the SqlEntityListener.onEnd() flush() to attempt to persist a RecordFile with a duplicate consensusEnd primary key, causing a database constraint violation that rolls back the entire @Transactional context and prevents any transactions in that file from being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted RecordFile whose name field contains path traversal characters (e.g., '../'), causing the Utility.archiveFile() call in the downloader to write the file to an unintended directory on the filesystem, potentially overwriting critical files?
